---
title: "Streaming Responses"
guide-section: "Architecture"
---

# Streaming Responses

The backend streams responses in real time so the user sees an answer form as it
is generated, rather than waiting for the full completion.

::: {#fig-streaming}
```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
    participant UI as React Frontend
    participant API as Flask API
    participant Gemini
    participant RAG as Vertex AI RAG

    UI->>API: POST /api/query with user message
    API->>API: Add user message to session
    API->>Gemini: Generate with stream=True + history
    Gemini->>RAG: Tool call: retrieve documents
    RAG-->>Gemini: Return legal passages

    loop Streaming response
        Gemini-->>API: Yield text / reasoning chunk
        API-->>UI: Stream chunk over the response body
        UI->>UI: Update message content incrementally
    end

    API->>API: Concatenate full response & update session
```

Chunks stream from Gemini through Flask to the browser as they are generated.
:::

## Response chunk types

Each line the backend writes to the response body is one serialized
[`ResponseChunk`](../reference/schema.ResponseChunk.qmd) — a discriminated union
distinguished by its `type` field:

- [`TextChunk`](../reference/schema.TextChunk.qmd) — a fragment of the answer text.
- [`ReasoningChunk`](../reference/schema.ReasoningChunk.qmd) — a model "thought",
  surfaced only when `SHOW_MODEL_THINKING` is enabled.
- [`LetterChunk`](../reference/schema.LetterChunk.qmd) — completed letter content
  emitted by the `generate_letter` tool, rendered in the letter panel rather than
  inline.
- [`EndOfStreamChunk`](../reference/schema.EndOfStreamChunk.qmd) — signals clean
  completion; it triggers the frontend's `onDone` callback and is not appended to
  the message content.

::: {.callout-important}
These Pydantic models are the source of truth for the frontend's TypeScript
types — `mise run generate-types` regenerates `frontend/src/types/models.ts` from
them, so the chunk contract stays in sync on both sides. Change a chunk here and
you must regenerate before the frontend build will type-check.
:::

## Backend mechanics

[`ChatView`](../reference/chat.ChatView.qmd) drives the stream. It calls
[`LangChainChatManager.generate_streaming_response`](../reference/langchain_chat_manager.LangChainChatManager.qmd),
which streams the agent in `["updates", "custom"]` mode: model text and reasoning
arrive as `updates`, while tool-emitted content (the letter) arrives as `custom`
chunks. Raw LangChain content blocks are classified into the typed chunks above,
each serialized as one newline-delimited JSON object. The manager retries a reset
connection up to twice, but never after any output has been yielded, to avoid
sending the client duplicate content.

::: {.callout-note}
The response uses `text/plain` because the client reads raw bytes off the stream
rather than parsing a media type.
:::

## Frontend consumption

The frontend reads the stream with the native `ReadableStream` API via
`response.body.getReader()`. Text chunks are appended incrementally; letter
chunks are extracted into the letter panel; the `end_of_stream` chunk ends the
read. See the frontend documentation for `streamHelper.ts`.

## Where to go next

- [Conversation Management](05-conversation-management.qmd) — how history persists
  across turns.
